response:
Sec. 1  
[TO DETERMINE WHEN PUBLISHER A'S OFFER BECOMES MORE LUCRATIVE THAN PUBLISHER B'S, WE NEED TO COMPARE THE TOTAL EARNINGS FROM BOTH PUBLISHERS.]

Sec. 2  
[THE EARNINGS FROM PUBLISHER A CAN BE REPRESENTED AS 3000 PLUS 5% OF THE TOTAL SALES REVENUE, WHICH IS 3000 + 0.05X.]

Sec. 3  
[THE EARNINGS FROM PUBLISHER B CAN BE REPRESENTED AS 1500 PLUS 10% OF THE TOTAL SALES REVENUE, WHICH IS 1500 + 0.10X.]

Sec. 4  
[TO FIND WHEN PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE, WE SET UP THE INEQUALITY 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X.]

Sec. 5  
[SOLVING THE INEQUALITY INVOLVES SUBTRACTING 1500 FROM BOTH SIDES, RESULTING IN 1500 + 0.05X > 0.10X.]

Sec. 6  
[SUBTRACTING 0.05X FROM BOTH SIDES YIELDS 1500 > 0.05X, WHICH SIMPLIFIES TO X < 30000.]

Sec. 7  
[THE RANGE OF SALES REVENUE WHERE PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE IS X LESS THAN 30000.]

Sec. 8  
[WITHIN THE ANTICIPATED RANGE OF 10000 TO 40000, PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE WHEN SALES REVENUE IS BETWEEN 10000 AND 30000.]
